project.name: JSummer
project.description: Ant buildfile for JSummer (c) Klaus Zerwes zero-sys.net 2005 - 2008 required: Apache Ant version 1.6.2 type 'ant help' or 'ant -p' for a list of targets

target.name: help-compiler
target.echo:
Interesting properties : you may adjust them to your needs using the ant-option -D<property>=<value>
java-compiler executable: - package.javacexec [${package.javacexec}] gcj native comiler executable: - package.gcjexec [${package.gcjexec}]
java compiler target / source version - package.java.targetversion [${package.java.targetversion}] - package.java.sourceversion [${package.java.sourceversion}] additional java compiler arguments - package.java.optimize [${package.java.optimize}]
native compiler debug-value (0 - min; 3 - max) - package.native.dbg [${package.native.dbg}] native compiler optimization-value (0 - min; 3 - max) - package.native.opt [${package.native.opt}]

target.name: help-swt
target.echo:
Interesting properties : you may adjust them to your needs using the ant-option -D<property>=<value>
swt-locations - package.swt.location [${package.swt.location}] - package.swt.src [${package.swt.src}] - package.swt.jar [${package.swt.jar}]
swt-lib - package.swtsharedlibname [${package.swtsharedlibname}] - package.swtsharedlibname is build from: swt-[package.swtsharedlibaddition]-[package.swt.version]-[package.swt.platform] package.swtsharedlibaddition [${package.swtsharedlibaddition}] package.swt.version and package.swt.platform are set dynamical - package.swtsharedlibpath [${package.swtsharedlibpath}]

target.name: help-install
target.echo:
Interesting properties : you may adjust them to your needs using the ant-option -D<property>=<value>
install-paths - package.installdir.base [${package.installdir.base}] - package.installdir.bin [${package.installdir.bin}] - package.installdir.lib [${package.installdir.lib}] - package.installdir.jar [${package.installdir.jar}]
for jar-install: the properties package.swt.jar and package.classpathadd are used to build the classpath for a simple installscript.

target.name: help-classpath
target.echo:
Interesting properties : you may adjust them to your needs using the ant-option -D<property>=<value>
package.classpathadd: the value of this property will be added as a prefix to the classpath package.swt.jar [${package.swt.jar}]

target.name: help-build-java
target.echo:
The main targets to build tha java-app are: jar jar-install
maybe helpfull properties: package.swt.location package.swt.jar package.installdir.base package.installdir.bin package.installdir.jar package.classpathadd

target.name: help-build-native-shared
target.echo:
How to build a native app linked against the swt-lib?
targets: native-shared-gui native-console native-install
expects swt-jar in '${package.swt.jar}'
example: ant -Dpackage.swt.jar=/usr/share/java/swt-zerosys-3139-gtk.jar \ -Dpackage.swtsharedlibpath=/usr/lib \ -Dpackage.swtsharedlibname=swt-zerosys-3139-gtk \ native-shared-gui
helpfull properties: package.swtsharedlibpath package.swtsharedlibname package.swt.location package.swt.jar package.swt.src package.installdir.base package.installdir.bin package.installdir.lib

target.name: help-build-native-static
target.echo:
How to build a native app including swt-stuff from source?
targets: native-static native-install
This build-type expects the swt-stuff in '${package.swt.location}'. This means the the swt-jar in '${package.swt.jar}' and the swt-src unpacked in '${package.swt.src}'.
helpfull properties: package.swt.location package.swt.jar package.swt.src package.installdir.base package.installdir.bin

target.name: help

echo.message: type 'ant -p' for a list of targets

target.echo:
help-targets for setting properties: - help-compiler properties affecting the compilers - help-swt properties affecting SWT - help-install properties affecting install-targets - help-classpath properties affecting classpath - problems report possible problems
help-targets for building: - help-build-java - help-build-native-shared - help-build-native-static

echo.message: please tell me the path to swt.jar using package.swt.jar
(exept you use a lib from your system)
please tell me the path to swt.jar using package.swt.src

target.name: jar-install
target.description: installs jar and builds a simple startupscript

fail.message: unable to find jar! first run ant jar!
unable to find ${package.executable.console}; please build it first!
unable to find ${package.executable.gui}; please build it first!

target.name: jar-guiserver
target.description: jar of the gui-app including server

target.name: native-shared-gui
target.description: native gui linked against external swt-lib

target.name: swtsharedcompile
target.description: compile swt to native for building a shared lib

target.name: swtsharedlink
target.description: compile swt to native for building a shared lib
